sourcePage.rawText:
18   The equation x 4 + bx 2 + c = 0 has four distinct real roots if and only if which of the following
     conditions is satisfied?

     A   b 2 > 4c

     B   b 2 < 4c

     C   c > 0 and b > 2 c

     D   c > 0 and b < −2 c

     E   c < 0 and b < 0

     F   c < 0 and b > 0




                                                  20

stem.rawText:
The equation x 4 + bx 2 + c = 0 has four distinct real roots if and only if which of the following
     conditions is satisfied?

solution.rawText:
Writing u = x2 , the equation becomes u2 + bu + c = 0. This has two distinct real roots u1 and u2
if and only if b2 − 4c > 0, or b2 > 4c, so this is a necessary condition.
                                                                 √              √
The roots of the original equation are then given by x = ± u1 and x = ± u2 . So for the
original equation to have four distinct real roots, we require u1 and u2 to be distinct positive
real numbers. We have                                 √
                                                 −b ± b2 − 4c
                                      u1 , u2 =
                                                       2
                             1
                                     √          
so the smaller of the two is 2 −b − b2 − 4c . If b > 0, then this will be negative, so we require
                                                    √
b < 0. Supposing b < 0, we also require −b > b2 − 4c, which becomes on squaring (as both
sides are positive) b2 > b2 − 4c, so c > 0.
Putting these all together, the original equation will have four distinct real roots if and only if
all of the following hold:

•    b2 > 4c

•    b<0

•    c>0
                                                               √            √
As c > 0, we can deduce that b2 > 4c if and only if b > 2 c or b < −2 c. Since we require
b < 0, the first is not possible, so the pair of conditions b < 0 and b2 > 4c is equivalent to the
                         √
single condition b < −2 c.
Therefore a necessary and sufficient condition for the original equation to have four distinct real
                         √
roots is c > 0 and b < −2 c, which is option D.
